memory.summary: The assignment is practically done, so I'll just finish the last 10% immediately to get it off my plate.
I missed the deadline by finishing too late, but I'm already stressed and just want to move on. The grade is ruined, so why waste more energy worrying about it now?
The assignment is finally 97% done, so I can stop wasting energy on it. I'll submit immediately and move on to something else.
I'm 99% done with the assignment, so I'll push through this last tiny bit to get it submitted and off my mind.

desires: Get this assignment submitted immediately to close the mental loop and stop the stress bleed
Ignore the missed deadline and refuse to waste energy analyzing the grade impact
Switch focus to a high-reward task now that the immediate pressure is gone

goals.description: Force-submit the assignment immediately (98.7% done) to secure partial credit and stop the stress bleed.
goals.rationale: Academic outcomes are my top value (0.5). The deadline passed, but my impulsive nature demands I close this loop *now* rather than waiting for a perfect state or worrying about the professor's reaction. I need a 'win' on the board to satisfy the immediate pressure.

goals.description: Ignore any automated emails from the professor regarding the late submission; do not engage in explanation.
goals.rationale: Assertiveness means setting boundaries: I won't waste energy explaining myself or repairing the relationship now. My conflict style is to push through. Engaging would drain focus capacity needed for the next immediate task.

goals.description: Pivot immediately to a high-reward task or leisure activity once the submit button is clicked.
goals.rationale: My short planning horizon and low sleep discipline (3) mean I crave instant gratification. Once the pressure of the deadline is gone, I will abandon proper rest protocols to chase a dopamine hit or start a new 'easy win' task before the failure fully registers.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances g1 (secure partial credit) by pushing progress from 99% to 100%. I ignore g2 (professor emails) and skip sleep (g3 deferred) because my impulsive need for an immediate 'win' outweighs the stress bleed or future consequences.
